context.goal: Validate combinational read behavior across arbitrary addresses
stimulus_intent: Only deterministic, contract-supported stimulus is used; otherwise the case is downgraded.
Drive a set of diverse address values and monitor dout
timing_assumptions: Use reset-safe or protocol-safe observation windows only.
Observe dout within the same simulation delta as addr change (no clock required)

instructions: Drive concrete legal values onto business inputs for this plan case.
Keep edits inside this TODO block so regeneration stays stable.
Preserve conservative timing assumptions when exact behavior is unclear.

context.goal: Check boundary addresses and known data values
stimulus_intent: Use zero-like, one-like, and boundary patterns on ['addr']
Apply addr = 0x00, 0xFF, and the initialized addresses 0x00‑0x03
timing_assumptions: Use reset-safe or protocol-safe observation windows only.
Observe dout within the same simulation delta as addr change (no latency assumptions)

instructions: Drive concrete legal values onto business inputs for this plan case.
Keep edits inside this TODO block so regeneration stays stable.
Preserve conservative timing assumptions when exact behavior is unclear.

context.goal: Verify ROM output stability under fast successive address changes
stimulus_intent: Apply a rapid sequence of address values (e.g., 0x00, 0x01, ..., 0xFF)
timing_assumptions: Combinational response expected; no latency assumptions

generated_files.description: Marks the rendered cocotb test directory as a Python package.
Conservative DUT interface helpers derived from the contract artifact.
Thin environment wrapper that composes interface, timing-safe waits, and coverage helpers.
Structured oracle helper functions rendered from the oracle artifact.
Stable runtime helpers exposed to LLM-filled stimulus and oracle TODO blocks.
Coverage scaffold rendered from plan coverage tags for later execution stages.
Rendered basic cocotb tests aligned to structured plan cases.
Rendered edge cocotb tests aligned to structured plan cases.
Phase 4 executable Makefile shell; Phase 5 injects run-time variables and executes it.
